content: Evidence for primary care effectiveness is robust: Countries with stronger primary care orientation have 20-30% lower all-cause mortality, 15-25% lower cardiovascular mortality, and better self-rated health. Within countries, regions with higher primary care physician density show lower rates of low birth weight, asthma hospitalizations, and diabetes complications. Continuity of care (seeing same provider >75% of visits) associated with 25% lower mortality, 20% fewer ED visits, and better medication adherence. The 'primary care effect' persists after adjusting for socioeconomic factors, suggesting independent contribution through early detection, coordinated management, and trust-based relationships enabling behavior change.
